what_it_does: Defines the navigable map for the language-DNA domain, listing repository areas, registry faces, engine proofs, governing laws, build-loop state, open tensions, learnings, and pointers to deeper documents.
observations: It frames itself as a pointers-not-copies atlas for the domain.
It describes the system as a generative bimodal compositional language built from tokens, registries, rules, and resolved addresses.
It maps the post-reorg directory structure and the roles of dna, engine, ui, mockups, dist, docs, archive, content, data, icons, source, reference, and root files.
It names 20 registry faces and gives a one-line description of each.
It documents seven engine proofs run by ./engine/rebuild.sh.
It records a typed-edge law: a valid typed edge is a verb with an equal opposite direction.
It collects recurring laws such as derivation over instantiation, meaning as difference, local resolution, and identity not equaling content.
It tracks build-loop criteria, rule-core campaign state, estate rollout completion, deferred work, and future backlog locations.
It includes open tensions and an append-only learnings ledger from prior work.
It ends with pointers to files that hold deeper orientation, lineage, canon, resolution model, loop docs, command packets, and onboarding.
standouts: The file treats documentation, build state, design law, and operational memory as one navigational surface.
The resolution model is described with four signed hierarchy axes and two mechanisms, cascade and compose.
The learnings ledger turns implementation incidents into durable process rules.
The map repeatedly states that depth should live in referenced files rather than be copied here.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with short explanatory prose under each section.
Uses tables for directory roles and build-loop status.
Uses bold emphasis and uppercase labels for command-state, law-state, and deferred work.
Uses path references as navigational anchors rather than embedding deep content.
Uses append-style bullets for durable lessons and live tensions.
Includes status glyphs and non-ASCII punctuation despite being a documentation map.
concerns: The file mixes stable orientation, current status, open tensions, and historical lessons, so freshness is difficult to assess from the file alone.
Several time-sensitive build-state claims appear without a file-local last-updated marker.
The stated pointers-not-copies rule is under pressure because some sections contain substantial compressed status detail.
notes: Brief someone on this as the project atlas: it explains the domain model, where things live, what laws govern them, what has been built, and where deeper records are found.
questions: What date was this map last updated?
Which sections are intended to be durable orientation versus live operational status?
How should completed campaign status be retired or refreshed in this file?
What is the expected update rule for the learnings ledger?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it acts as a navigable center that points to structure, laws, build state, lessons, tensions, and deeper documents.
intention_signals: It appears intended to preserve orientation for amnesiac sessions by compressing the domain’s structure, laws, status, unresolved questions, and reading order into one entry file.
novelty: It is unusual as a repo map because it combines architectural atlas, design philosophy, build ledger, command memory, and process doctrine in one document.
contribution: Contributes an orientation layer that names the domain, maps its file territories, records its governing laws and proof machinery, preserves live tensions and lessons, and points readers toward deeper sources without replacing them.
summary_for_embedding: A documentation atlas for counterpart-design’s language-DNA domain that maps repository structure, registry faces, engine proofs, type laws, build-loop status, open tensions, learnings, and deeper reference paths.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for keeping future sessions oriented by making the domain’s structure, laws, process state, and source-of-depth pointers visible in one place.
